title: Hieracium epirense
intro: Hieracium epirense is a rare species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its small yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium epirense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium epirense is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Hieracium epirense is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known as Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which consists of approximately 1000 species of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The plant is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is a relatively rare species within its genus. Its classification is further divided into the family Asteraceae, order Asterales, and class Magnoliopsida. As a member of the Hieracium genus, Hieracium epirense shares characteristics with other hawkweed species.
